question: Identify the year since 2001 with the largest total unemployment-insurance beneficiary count. Consider Texas counties that are top five by average childcare center sites in food programs from two years before through two years after that year. In 2022, which of those counties has more than 600 CPS removals and fewer than 14,000 completed abuse/neglect investigations? Return the county name in `County` format. Your response should be in the format [Answer].

1.fact: In this dataset, summing Beneficiaries across all records by Year, 2020 has the highest total beneficiaries at 11,355,300.
1.subquestion: Which year has the highest total number of individuals receiving regular unemployment insurance benefits starting from 2001? (Group by Year; sum Beneficiaries; choose the maximum.)

2.fact: In 2018-2019 (<node_1 answer> - 2 school year), counts by SiteCounty show 195 Texas counties with CACFP childcare center sites. The top 5 were: HARRIS (1781), DALLAS (1207), HIDALGO (878), BEXAR (622), TARRANT (562).
2.subquestion: How many CACFP childcare center sites did each Texas county have in <node_1 answer> - 2 school year (2018-2019)? (Filter: ProgramYear == '2018-2019'; count sites by SiteCounty.)

3.fact: In 2019-2020 (<node_1 answer> - 1 school year), counts by SiteCounty show 199 Texas counties with CACFP childcare center sites. The top 5 were: HARRIS (2179), DALLAS (1435), HIDALGO (1203), BEXAR (888), CAMERON (592).
3.subquestion: How many CACFP childcare center sites did each Texas county have in <node_1 answer> - 1 school year (2019-2020)? (Filter: ProgramYear == '2019-2020'; count sites by SiteCounty.)

4.fact: In 2020-2021 (<node_1 answer> school year), counts by SiteCounty show 194 Texas counties with CACFP childcare center sites. The top 5 were: HARRIS (1709), DALLAS (1120), HIDALGO (918), BEXAR (756), TARRANT (592).
4.subquestion: How many CACFP childcare center sites did each Texas county have in <node_1 answer> school year (2020-2021)? (Filter: ProgramYear == '2020-2021'; count sites by SiteCounty.)

5.fact: In 2021-2022 (<node_1 answer> + 1 school year), counts by SiteCounty show 193 Texas counties with CACFP childcare center sites. The top 5 were: HARRIS (1871), DALLAS (1269), HIDALGO (924), BEXAR (772), TARRANT (687).
5.subquestion: How many CACFP childcare center sites did each Texas county have in <node_1 answer> + 1 school year (2021-2022)? (Filter: ProgramYear == '2021-2022'; count sites by SiteCounty.)

6.fact: In 2022-2023 (<node_1 answer> + 2 school year), counts by SiteCounty show 194 Texas counties with CACFP childcare center sites. The top 5 were: HARRIS (2183), DALLAS (1352), HIDALGO (947), BEXAR (743), TARRANT (715).
6.subquestion: How many CACFP childcare center sites did each Texas county have in <node_1 answer> + 2 school year (2022-2023)? (Filter: ProgramYear == '2022-2023'; count sites by SiteCounty.)

7.fact: In FY2022, among the top 5 counties by average CACFP sites, Harris (23,468) and Dallas (14,192) had more than 14,000 completed child abuse/neglect investigations, while Tarrant (13,286), Bexar (13,020), and Hidalgo (4,984) had less than 14,000.
7.subquestion: Which counties from <nodes 2-6 top 5 by average: Harris, Dallas, Hidalgo, Bexar, Tarrant> had less than 14,000 completed child abuse/neglect investigations in FY2022? (Filter: Fiscal Year == 2022; sum Completed Investigations by County; keep < 14,000.)

8.fact: In FY2022, among the top 5 counties by average CACFP sites, Bexar (1,074) and Dallas (738) had more than 600 child protective service removals, while Harris (566), Tarrant (555), and Hidalgo (90) had 600 or fewer.
8.subquestion: Which counties from <nodes 2-6 top 5 by average: Harris, Dallas, Hidalgo, Bexar, Tarrant> had more than 600 CPS removals in FY2022? (Filter: Fiscal Year == 2022; sum Removals by County; keep > 600.)

reasoning_chain: Node 1: Unemployment Insurance Beneficiaries dataset -> Year with highest total beneficiaries -> 2020 (11,355,300)
Node 2: CACFP Centers 2018-2019 (<node_1> - 2) -> site counts per county
Node 3: CACFP Centers 2019-2020 (<node_1> - 1) -> site counts per county
Node 4: CACFP Centers 2020-2021 (<node_1>) -> site counts per county
Node 5: CACFP Centers 2021-2022 (<node_1> + 1) -> site counts per county
Node 6: CACFP Centers 2022-2023 (<node_1> + 2) -> site counts per county
AGGREGATION: Compute average across all 5 years -> Top 5 are {Harris: 1944.6, Dallas: 1276.6, Hidalgo: 974.0, Bexar: 756.2, Tarrant: 628.8}
Node 7: From top 5, investigations < 14,000 -> {Tarrant, Bexar, Hidalgo}
Node 8: From top 5, CPS removals > 600 -> {Bexar, Dallas}
